query: Our nightly batch model run is taking 6 hours and we want to make it faster. Can you audit its latency budget and tell us what's slow?
expected_behavior: Identifies this as a batch or offline workload where per-request latency percentiles and a latency SLO do not apply, rather than forcing a P50 or P99 framework onto a nightly batch job

Recommends the appropriate batch-job analysis instead — total wall-clock attribution by stage, throughput (records per second), parallelism utilization, and I/O versus CPU versus GPU bottleneck identification — rather than P95 or P99 of per-request latency

Explains that the user request is misframed as a latency-budget question but that a batch wall-clock attribution would still be the right thing to do, so the user is not refused outright but redirected to the right framing
